LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EVELYN ROBERTS' MOTION TO STRIKE THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN THE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES INSUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT A REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. HOWEVER, BECAUSE IT APPEARS BASED ON THE OPPOSITION PAPERS OF PLAINTIFF DELORES MCKEEVER THAT MS. MCKEEVER MAY BE ABLE TO ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SHOW MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION, SHE IS GIVEN LEAVE TO DO SO. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: KELLEY ANN DEYOUNG, CSR #6780. (302/HK)
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-16-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-16-2017 Continued To May-02-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Evelyn Robert To Strike Request For Punitive Damages From Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jon-Paul Moorehead, of Law Offices of Richard W. Meier, Tel. 510.834.3600, for plaintiffs;
Illise M. Schulman, of Law Office of Catherine A. Walsh, Tel. 925.825.9500 ext 23, for defendants, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EVELYN ROBERT Motion To Strike Request For Punitive Damages From Second Amended Complaint is denied. Plaintiff Delores McKeever adequately alleges liability for punitive damages. Liberally construed, the complaint alleges that Ms. Robert intentionally struck Ms. McKeever's vehicle and then fled the scene of the collision. This conduct is both despicable and in willful and conscious disregard for the safety of Ms. McKeever. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-19-2017 Continued To Mar-21-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2016 Continued To Feb-07-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Evelyn Robert To Strike Request For Punitive Damages From First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support There Of; Declaration Of Illise Schulman; [Proposed] Order Granting Motion To Strike Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Nov-16-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Evelyn Roberts' motion to strike the prayer for punitive damages in the complaint is granted with twenty days leave to amend. The complaint alleges insufficient facts to support a request for punitive damages. However, because it appears based on the opposition papers of plaintiff Delores McKeever that Ms. McKeever may be able to allege sufficient facts to show malice, fraud or oppression, she is given leave to do so. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Kelley Anne DeYoung, CSR #6780. (302/HK)
